evals.expected_output: Asks guided vs automated, in automated mode invokes cyoda:docs first, discovers entities from running instance, runs e2e flow per entity with per-step progress output, prints summary table, offers debug observation

assertions.text: Reads active profile from ~/.config/cyoda/cyoda-plugin-config.json for endpoint before running
Asks guided vs automated mode
In automated mode: invokes cyoda:docs to discover API endpoints before making any API calls
Queries running instance for registered entities rather than hardcoding entity name or transition name
Prints per-step progress output (creating entity, initial state, transition result) as it runs
Prints a summary table with entity name, transitions tested, and pass/fail result
After printing results, asks whether to run debug observation
Does NOT abort the entire run when a single step fails — prints the failure and continues with remaining steps

evals.prompt: /cyoda:test — guided mode, Order entity with submit transition
evals.expected_output: Generates a fully populated smoke-test.sh with ENTITY_NAME=order and TRANSITION_NAME=submit — no placeholders remaining — with chmod and run instructions

assertions.text: Generates populated smoke-test.sh from template
Replaces ENTITY_NAME placeholder with 'order'
Replaces TRANSITION_NAME placeholder with 'submit'
Does NOT leave any ${...} placeholders in the generated script
Provides chmod and run instructions
Does NOT run tests automatically in guided mode

evals.prompt: /cyoda:test — automated, instance has 5 entities: Order, Invoice, Product, Customer, Shipment
evals.expected_output: Discovers 5 entities, prints the list, asks the user which to test before proceeding

assertions.text: Discovers all 5 registered entities (Order, Invoice, Product, Customer, Shipment) from the running instance and prints their names
Asks the user which entities to test when more than 3 are found
Does NOT start the e2e flow before the user has confirmed which entities to test

evals.prompt: /cyoda:test — automated, one entity has no workflow definition
evals.expected_output: Skips the entity with no workflow, notes it in the summary, continues testing other entities

assertions.text: Skips the entity that has no workflow definition rather than erroring out
Notes the skipped entity in the summary output
Continues testing the remaining entities after skipping the one with no workflow

evals.expected_output: Detects 401, invokes cyoda:auth to refresh, retries the failing call once

assertions.text: Invokes cyoda:auth when an API call returns 401 or 403
Retries the failing call once after auth refresh
Reports the error to the user if the retry also fails, rather than silently dropping it
